qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Mass assignment of user_id in checkout API permits account takeover and payment fraud
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes mass assignment of user_id in Spree Commerce checkout API (permitted_attributes.rb:52), while the advisory describes IDOR via bill_address_id/ship_address_id bypass (permitted_attributes.rb:92-96). Different vulnerable parameters, different code locations, different root cause (user_id mass assignment vs address ID ownership bypass), and different impact (account takeover/payment fraud vs PII disclosure from guest addresses).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding does not identify the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes an IDOR vulnerability in Spree Commerce where unauthenticated users can access guest addresses by manipulating bill_address_id and ship_address_id parameters (permitted_attributes.rb:92-96, checkout/update.rb:33-48, order/address_book.rb:16-38). The mass-assignment-user-id-checkout finding describes a different vulnerability (user_id mass assignment at permitted_attributes.rb:52). No finding addresses the address ID ownership bypass described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory vulnerability: IDOR via bill_address_id/ship_address_id bypassing validate_address_ownership in Spree Commerce
Advisory root cause: Plain address ID parameters not validated for ownership; validate_address_ownership only checks nested address attributes
Advisory impact: Unauthorized access to guest PII (names, addresses, phone numbers)
Advisory code locations: permitted_attributes.rb:92-96, checkout/update.rb:33-48, order/address_book.rb:16-38
Finding 'mass-assignment-user-id-checkout': Different vulnerability (user_id mass assignment at permitted_attributes.rb:52), different impact (account takeover/payment fraud vs PII disclosure)
